title: Facility Video Footage Request & Chain of Custody
content:
Facility Video Footage Request & Chain of Custody

SOP ID: SEC-SOP-002
Version: 6.0
Effective Date: 2025-08-07

1.0 Purpose & Scope

To establish a strict procedure for the request, export, and handling of sensitive video footage from the Genetec Security Center platform. This SOP ensures compliance with privacy policies and maintains the evidentiary chain of custody using Genetec's built-in security features.

This procedure applies to all requests for video footage made by authorized personnel and governs the actions of the System Administrator responsible for the export.

2.0 Procedure

2.1 Roles & Responsibilities

  • Requestor: An authorized manager or HR representative.
  • Approver: A designated senior leader (e.g., Head of HR, Director of Operations).
  • System Administrator (3): Designated IT personnel with export privileges in Genetec Security Desk. Each uses their own unique credentials.
  • Legal Counsel: Must be consulted on any request where footage is intended for legal proceedings.

2.2 High-Level Workflow

1. Request Submitted
2. Approval (Legal if needed)
3. Secure Export & Hash
4. Secure Transfer

2.3 Footage Request & Export Procedure

1

Step 1: Formal Request Submission

The Requestor must complete the "Chain of Custody Form" (Section 2.6), specifying the nature of the request, reason, date, time range, camera(s), and if legal involvement is anticipated. The form is submitted to the Approver.

2

Step 2: Approval or Denial

The Approver reviews the request. If "Legal Involvement Required: Yes" is checked, the procedure in Section 2.4 must be followed before proceeding. The approved form is then forwarded to the System Administrator.

3

Step 3: Footage Export from Security Desk

The System Administrator receives the approved form and performs the export within the Genetec Security Desk application:

  1. Log In and Navigate: Log into Genetec Security Desk at https://192.168.1.100/SecurityDesk. From the home page, open the Archives task.
  2. Locate and Select Footage: In the entity tree (top-left), select the required camera. Use the timeline at the bottom to navigate to the incident date and time. Zoom in on the timeline for precision.
  3. Define Export Time Range: Play the video to find the exact start of the event and click the "Set start time" button (🏁+). Continue to the end of the event and click the "Set end time" button. The selected range will be highlighted.
  4. Initiate and Configure Export: Click the "Export video" button (💾). In the "Export settings" dialog box:
    • Set File format to G64x.
    • Check "Add password protection" and enter a strong, unique password.
    • Edit the Filename to match the required convention: `YYYY-MM-DD_CameraName_CaseID.g64x`.
    Strong Password Requirement: A strong password must be at least 14 characters long and include uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols (e.g., `!@#$%^&*`). It must not be a previously used password.
  5. Complete Export and Calculate Hash: Click the Export button. Once the export is complete and available in the Vault, calculate an external SHA-256 hash of the final file.
    What does "available in the Vault" mean? The Genetec Vault is a secure, local folder on the administrator's workstation where Security Desk saves all exports, snapshots, and other evidence. This ensures all exported evidence is centrally managed in one location on the local machine before being transferred.
    What is a SHA-256 Hash? A SHA-256 hash is a unique digital fingerprint of a file. Even a tiny change to the file will produce a completely different hash. This is critical for proving that the exported video evidence has not been tampered with. To calculate it, open Windows PowerShell, navigate to the file's location, and run the command:
    certutil -hashfile "YYYY-MM-DD_CameraName_CaseID.g64x" SHA256
4

Step 4: Secure Transfer & Documentation

The System Administrator will:

  1. Transfer the G64x file and the completed Chain of Custody form to the Requestor via a company-issued, BitLocker-encrypted USB drive. Provide the password separately via a secure channel (e.g., secure chat, phone call).
    2. How to Encrypt a USB Drive with BitLocker:
    1. Insert the USB drive into your computer.
    2. Open File Explorer, right-click the USB drive, and select "Turn on BitLocker".
    3. Choose "Use a password to unlock the drive", enter a strong password, and click Next.
    4. Save the recovery key to a secure location (e.g., print it and store in a physical safe). This is crucial for data recovery if the password is lost.
    5. Choose "Encrypt used disk space only" and "Compatible mode".
    6. Click "Start encrypting". The drive is now secure.
  2. The Requestor signs the Chain of Custody form, acknowledging receipt.
  3. The System Administrator files the fully signed forms in a secure, access-controlled digital folder, for example:
    \\corp-fs01\SecureDocs\ChainOfCustody\

2.4 Legal Involvement Protocol

When "Legal Involvement Required: Yes" is checked on the request form, the System Administrator must halt the export process until they have contacted Legal Counsel. The following questions must be asked to ensure proper evidence handling:

  • What is the required time frame for the footage? How much time before and after the specified incident should be included?
  • Are there any other cameras or angles that need to be included in this export?
  • How many separate, secured USB copies of the evidence are required?
  • Are there any specific individuals who should NOT have access to this footage?
  • Is there a legal hold on this evidence, and if so, what is the required retention period?

The answers to these questions must be documented and attached to the Chain of Custody form. Do not proceed with the export until written guidance is received from Legal Counsel.

2.5 Data Integrity & Retention Policy

Confidentiality and Security Warning

Exported footage is highly sensitive. Genetec's digital signature and password protection features must be used for all exports. Unless part of an ongoing legal hold, all exported footage must be securely destroyed no more than 90 days after the associated investigation is formally closed.

2.6 Chain of Custody Form (Template)

This form must accompany all exported footage.

Case / Incident ID:
Legal Involvement Required: Yes No
If Yes, Contact Legal Counsel:

Date Contacted: _______________
Counsel Name: _______________

Request Approved By:
Date/Time of Export:
Exported By (Admin Name):
File Name:
SHA-256 Hash:
Custody Transfer #1:Released By (Admin):
Signature: ___________________
Date/Time: _______________

Received By (Requestor):
Signature: ___________________
Date/Time: _______________
Final Disposition of Footage:
(e.g., Archived, Deleted per policy)
Date of Disposition:
Required Destruction Date:

3.0 Document Info

Related Documents

This section lists any other documents, policies, or procedures that are relevant to or referenced within this SOP.

  • [Related Document 1: Corporate Privacy Policy]
  • [Related Document 2: Incident Response Plan]

Revision History

This section tracks changes made to the SOP over time, including the date of revision, a brief description of the change, and the name of the individual who made the change.

DateVersionDescription of ChangeAuthor
2025-08-076.0Added color, icons, and improved visual hierarchy.Jonathan R.

title: Troubleshooting Badge Printer Network Issues
content:
Troubleshooting Badge Printer Network Issues

SOP ID: SOP-IT-004
Version: 1.5
Effective Date: 2025-08-06

1.0 Purpose & Scope

To provide a systematic, tiered checklist for both Front Desk and IT staff to efficiently diagnose and resolve network connectivity issues with Brother Badge Printers used with the Envoy Visitor Management System, minimizing downtime.

This procedure applies to all personnel responding to badge printing failures. It is validated for the following network-capable Brother models:

  • Brother QL-810W
  • Brother QL-820NWB
  • (Add other company-approved models here)

2.0 Procedure

2.1 Diagnostic Workflow

graph TD;A[Start: Report of Printer Failure] ---> B[Part 1: Front Desk Checks];B -- Issue Resolved ---> F[End];B -- Issue Persists ---> C[Escalate to IT];C ---> D[Part 2: IT Diagnostics];D -- Issue Resolved ---> E[Document & Close Ticket];D -- Issue Persists ---> G[Escalate to Network Engineering];G ---> E;

2.2 Part 1: For Front Desk Staff (Levels 1 & 2)

These initial steps can be performed by front desk or facilities staff to resolve common issues before escalating to IT. If the printer is still not working after completing all steps in Part 1, please submit an IT ticket.

Level 1: Initial Triage & Physical/Mechanical Checks 🕵️‍♀️

Level 2: Basic Connection Checks 🌐

2.3 Part 2: For IT Support (Levels 3 & 4)

These advanced steps require IT access and knowledge. Begin here after the Front Desk has completed Part 1.

Level 3: Advanced Network & Envoy Configuration ⚙️

⚠️ These steps can impact network settings. Proceed with caution.

Preventative Action: DHCP Reservation
To prevent future IP mismatches, create a DHCP reservation for the printer's MAC address (found on the config printout) in your network controller. This is the preferred method over setting a static IP on the device itself.

Level 4: Escalation & Final Checks 📞

3.0 Document Info

Related Documents

This section lists any other documents, policies, or procedures that are relevant to or referenced within this SOP.

  • [Related Document 1: Corporate Network Policy]
  • [Related Document 2: IT Ticketing Procedure]

Revision History

This section tracks changes made to the SOP over time, including the date of revision, a brief description of the change, and the name of the individual who made the change.

DateVersionDescription of ChangeAuthor
2025-08-061.5Added more detailed technical checks and clarified wording.IT Support Team

Approval

Date: _________________________

title: Sourcing & Vetting New Subcontractors
content:
Sourcing & Vetting New Subcontractors

SOP ID: PM-SOP-001
Version: 1.1
Effective Date: 2024-10-27

1.0 Purpose & Scope

To establish a standardized, repeatable process for expanding our network of qualified subcontractors. This ensures that as we enter new markets or take on new types of work, we partner with reliable, financially stable, and skilled trades who align with our quality and safety standards.

2.0 Procedure

2.1 Roles & Responsibilities (RACI Chart)

A RACI chart is a project management tool used to clarify roles and responsibilities for each task in a process, eliminating confusion and enhancing accountability. By defining who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed, we ensure that every step has a clear owner and that all necessary stakeholders are included at the appropriate time.

Process StepProject ManagerOperations ManagerFinance Dept.Safety Dept.
Define Subcontractor NeedsRRACC
Source & Screen CandidatesRRACS
Conduct Vetting & ScoringRRACS
Final Selection & ApprovalRRAIR
Onboard to CRM & SystemsRSACC
Key:A=Accountable,R=Responsible,C=Consulted,I=Informed,S=Support,RA=Responsible & Accountable,SA=Support & Accountable

2.2 Vetting Workflow Overview

1. Sourcing
2. Screening
3. Due Diligence
4. Approval
5. Onboarding

2.3 Step-by-Step Procedure

As you go through each step, fill out the Vetting Scorecard Calculator (Section 2.4) to decide if the subcontractor passes our minimum score.

1

Step 1: Identification & Sourcing

Proactively identify potential subcontractors in target areas or specialties.

  • Industry Associations: Engage with local chapters of trade associations (e.g., ABC, AGC).
  • Supplier Recommendations: Ask for referrals from trusted material suppliers in the new region.
  • Online Platforms: Utilize platforms like The Blue Book, BuildingConnected, or even LinkedIn.
  • Peer Referrals: Network with other GCs or developers for recommendations.
2

Step 2: Initial Screening & Pre-Qualification

Conduct a preliminary review to filter out non-viable candidates. As you complete these checks, enter the corresponding scores into the interactive scorecard below.

  • Send a standardized Pre-Qualification Questionnaire.
  • Verify basic licensing and insurance capabilities online via state licensing boards.
  • Review their website and portfolio for relevance and quality.
  • Verify legal standing: Check for any pending, current, or previous lawsuits through public court records.
  • Check for any immediate red flags (e.g., negative online reviews, expired licenses).
3

Step 3: Deep-Dive Vetting & Due Diligence

For candidates who pass screening, conduct a thorough investigation and complete the remaining sections of the scorecard.

  • Reference Checks: Contact at least 3 recent clients (preferably other GCs) using a standardized list of questions.
  • Quality of Work Review: Visit at least one current and one recently completed job site to assess workmanship, safety, and site cleanliness.
  • Knowledge & Competency Interview: A knowledgeable team member (e.g., Project Manager) will meet with the subcontractor's PM or Foreman to test their knowledge. This can be done by reviewing a current job site and asking detailed questions, or by discussing specific "how-to" scenarios for a potential project.
4

Step 4: Final Review & Approval

A multi-departmental review ensures a holistic approval decision based on the final scorecard result.

  • The Project Manager finalizes the Vetting Scorecard. A minimum score of 3.5 is required to proceed.
  • The completed package is reviewed by the heads of Operations, Finance, and Safety, who provide a formal sign-off.
  • Upon unanimous approval, the subcontractor is added to the "Approved Vendor List."
5

Step 5: Onboarding & System Integration

Properly integrate the newly approved subcontractor into our systems.

  • Send a welcome packet with our company policies, billing procedures, and key contacts.
  • Per the RACI chart the Project manager and OM will enter all subcontractor info in CRM within Clickup.
  • Ensure their COI and W-9 are on file before any work is awarded.
How to Access ClickUp: Navigate to clickup.com, click 'Sign In', and use your Google Workspace account to log in, as the accounts are connected.

2.4 Interactive Vetting Scorecard

The Subcontractor Vetting Scorecard is a data-driven tool designed to standardize and remove subjectivity from the evaluation process. It uses a weighted system to focus on the most critical risk factors. As you complete the procedural steps for each criterion above, enter a score from 0-5. The final calculated score will determine the approval decision. A subcontractor must meet the minimum passing score of 3.5 to be considered for partnership.

Vetting CriterionWeightScore (0-5)Weighted Score
Business Stability & History20%0.00
Insurance & Licensing Compliance25%0.00
Legal & Reputational Check15%0.00
Reference Checks15%0.00
Quality & Competency Review15%0.00
Communication & Professionalism10%0.00
FINAL WEIGHTED SCORE0.00

Approval Status:

Awaiting Score...

Approve: Score ≥ 4.0 | Approve with Caution: 3.5 ≤ Score < 4.0 | Reject: Score < 3.5

2.5 Documentation & Systems

  • All vetting documentation (questionnaires, financials, safety records, etc.) must be stored on the company's shared drive under: `Vetting > [Subcontractor Name] > [Year]`.
  • The master "Approved Vendor List" is maintained within the company's CRM and is managed by the Operations Manager.

3.0 Document Info

Related Documents

This section lists any other documents, policies, or procedures that are relevant to or referenced within this SOP.

  • [Related Document 1: Master Service Agreement Template]
  • [Related Document 2: Corporate Safety Policy]

Revision History

2024-10-271.1Updated RACI chart and added ClickUp login details.Jonathan R.

Approval

title: Issuing a Part to a Work Order
content:
Issuing a Part to a Work Order

SOP ID: RD-INV-001
Version: 2.1
Effective Date: August 4, 2025

1.0 Purpose & Scope

Purpose: To define the standardized procedure for issuing parts from inventory to a specific work order using the eMaint CMMS. This process is essential for maintaining accurate, real-time inventory counts, which prevents production delays and equipment downtime caused by unavailable parts. Furthermore, it ensures costs are assigned correctly to projects and maintains data integrity for forecasting and auditing.

2.0 Procedure

2.1 Prerequisites & Required Materials

  • An active and authorized user account for the eMaint CMMS. (If you need an account or require a password reset, contact the IT Help Desk).
  • A dedicated workstation with reliable internet access.
  • A functional and properly configured USB or Bluetooth barcode scanner.
  • A valid and open Work Order (WO) number, which will be provided on the request form or by the Project Lead.
  • The physical part(s) being requested.

2.2 Visual Process Flowchart

Start
Login & Navigate
Scan Part

(or Enter Manually)

Sufficient
Quantity?
Escalate to Stockroom Mgr
End (Halted)
Enter Qty, WO#, Asset & Cost Center
Review & Save
End (Issued)

2.3 Step-by-Step Procedure

1

Step 1: Log in & Navigate

Launch the eMaint application by navigating to https://YourEMaintURL.com in your web browser. Enter your company-provided username and password. If you cannot locate your credentials, contact your supervisor. Once logged in, use the main navigation menu and click on Inventory, then select Issue Parts from the dropdown menu.

2

Step 2: Identify the Part

Click your cursor into the "Bin Location" or "Part Number" field and scan the barcode on the inventory bin. The Part Number, Description, and Quantity on Hand fields will automatically fill.

3

Step 3: Enter Transaction Details

After scanning the part, you must verify its identity and enter the transaction details. Follow these sub-steps carefully:

3a. Verify the Part

Before entering any data, perform this critical check: Look at the Description field that auto-populated on the screen. Visually inspect the physical part in your hand. Ensure the description on the screen perfectly matches the physical part. If it does not, do not proceed. Escalate this as a "Part Description Mismatch" using the troubleshooting guide in section 2.4.

3b. Fill in Transaction Fields

Once the part is verified, accurately fill in the following fields:

  • Quantity: Enter the number of units you are issuing.
  • Work Order: Type the full Work Order number provided by the requestor. The system should validate the number; if an error appears, double-check for typos.
  • Asset (If Applicable): If the Work Order specifies a particular asset (e.g., 'Pump-101'), click the dropdown and select that asset. This is vital for accurate lifetime cost tracking. If no asset is specified, you may leave this blank.
  • Assign Charge To/Cost Center: Select the department or cost center that this part's cost should be allocated to. This information is typically found on the Work Order header. If you are unsure, do not guess. Consult the Project Lead to confirm the correct cost center before proceeding.
IMPORTANT: Double-check the "Unit of Issue" field (e.g., Each, Box, Ft.) displayed on the screen to ensure you are dispensing the correct amount. If the unit of issue is "Box" and you only need one item from it, refer to the troubleshooting guide.
4

Step 4: Finalize Transaction

Review all fields on the screen for accuracy. Click the "Save" or "Issue Part" button. Wait for the green "Transaction Saved Successfully" confirmation message before leaving the page.

Issuing Multiple Parts

To issue several different parts to the same Work Order, complete the process for the first part. Instead of clicking "Save," click the "Add New Line" button. This saves the current line and provides a new blank line to scan the next part. Repeat this process for all items. When you have added all items, click the "Save" button at the bottom of the page to finalize the entire transaction at once.

Note on Part Kits

For frequently repeated jobs, the Stockroom Manager may create "Part Kits." A kit bundles multiple different parts under a single part number or barcode. If your Work Order calls for a kit (e.g., 'PM-Kit-Pump-101'), you only need to scan the single barcode for the kit. The system will automatically issue all the pre-defined components to your Work Order, saving significant time.

2.4 Troubleshooting & Escalation

IssueFirst ResponseEscalate To
Barcode won't scan
  1. Confirm the scanner is on and properly connected. If USB, unplug it and plug it back in.
  2. Wipe the barcode label with a clean, dry cloth.
  3. Try scanning a different, known-good barcode to confirm the scanner is functional.
  4. If the scanner works but won't read the label, manually type the Part Number from the label into the 'Part Number' field.
Stockroom Mgr (if label is damaged)
"Insufficient Quantity" Error
  1. Immediately stop the transaction. Do not issue the part from the system.
  2. Perform a careful physical count of the items in the bin.
  3. Place a 'DO NOT USE - COUNT DISCREPANCY' tag or note on the bin.
  4. Immediately notify the Stockroom Manager of the discrepancy.
Stockroom Mgr (for count correction)
"Invalid Work Order" Error
  1. Carefully re-type the Work Order number, checking for common errors (e.g., 'O' vs '0').
  2. Verify the WO number against the official request form or email.
  3. If correct, contact the Project Lead to ask: "Can you confirm WO [number] is open and active in eMaint?"
Project Lead (to verify WO status)
Part Description Mismatch
  1. CRITICAL: Stop the transaction immediately. Do not issue the wrong part.
  2. Leave the physical part in its bin.
  3. Take a clear photo showing the physical part and the incorrect bin label.
  4. Report the issue to the Stockroom Manager for data correction.
Stockroom Mgr (to correct system data)
Wrong Unit of Issue
  1. Stop the transaction. Do not try to guess a fractional quantity (e.g., 0.1).
  2. Check for a separate bin location for individual units ("eaches") of the part.
  3. If none, ask the Stockroom Manager if the part can be "broken down" from a box and issued individually.
Stockroom Mgr (to confirm if part can be issued individually)
Scanner Enters Wrong Data
  1. Delete the incorrect data entered by the scanner.
  2. Manually type the Part Number exactly as it appears on the bin label.
  3. If manual entry still brings up the wrong part, the barcode is linked incorrectly.
  4. Treat this as a "Part Description Mismatch" and report it to the Stockroom Manager.
Stockroom Mgr (to verify barcode data)
Transaction Fails to Save / Button is Greyed Out
  1. Scan the form for any required fields marked in red or with an asterisk (*) that were missed.
  2. Perform a "hard refresh" of the page (Ctrl+F5) and try the transaction again.
  3. If it still fails, take a screenshot of the entire page (including any error messages).
  4. Provide the screenshot to IT Support, as this may be a permissions issue.
IT Support / eMaint Admin (provide a screenshot of the error)

2.5 Procedure for Returning Unused Parts

If you have unused parts from a Work Order that need to be returned to the stockroom, follow this procedure to ensure proper credit and inventory accuracy.

  1. Navigate to Inventory > Return Parts in the main menu.
  2. Scan the part being returned to populate its details.
  3. In the 'Quantity' field, enter the number of units you are returning.
  4. In the 'Return to Work Order' field, enter the original Work Order number the part was issued to. This is crucial for crediting the cost back correctly.
  5. Click 'Save' to confirm the return. The system will add the quantity back to the 'On Hand' inventory count.

3.0 Document Info

Related Documents

This section lists any other documents, policies, or procedures that are relevant to or referenced within this SOP.

  • [Related Document 1: Corporate Inventory Policy]
  • [Related Document 2: Work Order Management Procedure]

Revision History

This section tracks changes made to the SOP over time, including the date of revision, a brief description of the change, and the name of the individual who made the change.

DateVersionDescription of ChangeAuthor
August 4, 20252.1Initial release for eMaint system.R&D Operations / Field Operations Support

Approval

This section provides spaces for the necessary approvals to validate and authorize the SOP.

Approved By: _________________________ (Operations Manager)

Date: _________________________

title: Check-In System Implementation
content:
Check-In System Implementation

SOP ID: SOP-FAC-001
Version: 1.5
Effective Date: 2025-08-13

1.0 Purpose & Scope

To establish a standardized process for creating, updating, and synchronizing visitor sign-in templates across all company locations via Envoy. This ensures brand consistency, legal compliance with standardized documents (NDAs, waivers), and a uniform visitor experience.

2.0 Procedure

2.1 Definition of Terms

Global AdministratorA user with the highest level of permissions in Envoy, capable of managing settings for all locations.
Master TemplateA centralized set of branding, legal documents, and sign-in fields that can be applied to multiple Visitor Types across various locations.
Visitor TypeA category of visitor (e.g., "Client," "Interviewee," "Delivery") with a specific sign-in flow.
Location AdminA user with permissions to manage settings for one or more specific locations, but not all.

2.2 Prerequisites & Login

  • Admin Access: You must have Global Administrator privileges in Envoy. Access is restricted to authorized full-time staff only.
  • System Requirements: A modern, up-to-date web browser (e.g., Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge) is required for full dashboard functionality.
  • Content Approval: Ensure all legal documents have been approved by the Legal department and all branding assets (logos, colors) have been approved by the Marketing department prior to upload.
  • Content Ready: Have the latest approved versions of the company logo, brand colors (hex codes), and all required legal documents (in PDF format) ready.
    • File Naming: It is recommended to use a consistent naming convention for legal documents, such as `DocumentName_Scope_YYYY-MM-DD.pdf` (e.g., `NDA_Global_2025-08-13.pdf`).
  • Login Link: Access the Envoy Dashboard here: https://dashboard.envoy.com/login

2.3 Accessing and Creating the Master Template

💡 Key Concept: In Envoy, a Template is a master set of rules for branding and sign-in questions. This template is then applied to one or more Visitor Types (e.g., 'Client,' 'Interviewee'). This SOP focuses on creating the master Template.
1

Log into the Envoy Dashboard using your company credentials.

  • Only authorized full-time staff will have login credentials.
  • If you have forgotten your password, use the "Forgot password?" link on the login page.
  • If you are still unable to log in after a password reset, please contact the IT department for assistance.
2

In the left-hand navigation pane, go to Visitors → Visitor Types.

3

Click on the Templates tab at the top of the page. This view shows all existing templates.

4

Click the "Create template" button. Name it clearly using version control, e.g., "Global Master Template - v1.5".

Using a version number (e.g., v1.5) helps track changes over time and makes it easy to identify or revert to a previous version if necessary.

Missing "Create template" button?

If you do not see this option, you likely do not have the required Global Administrator permissions. Please contact the primary Envoy account holder or the IT department to request the appropriate access.

2.4 Configuring Standardized Branding

🎨 Goal: To ensure every visitor sees the same logo and color scheme, reinforcing the company brand.
5

Within the template editor, navigate to the Branding section.

6

Upload the official, approved company logo.

  • Recommended Size: For best results, use a high-resolution PNG file with a transparent background. The image should be at least 1000px on its longest side to ensure it appears sharp on all devices.
  • Verification: To ensure you have the correct, most up-to-date version of the logo, please contact the Branding/Marketing team.
7

Enter the primary brand color hex code for the buttons and accents.

2.5 Adding/Updating Legal Documents

⚖️ Goal: To ensure all visitors sign the correct, legally-approved documents, protecting the company.
8

Navigate to the Sign-in flow section and find the Legal documents block.

9

Upload the standardized NDA or Waiver PDF. Use a clear naming convention for version control, e.g., "Global NDA - 2025-Q4".

10

Configure the settings. Choose the Document Type (e.g., 'Sign on device' for a captured signature or 'Click-through' for simple agreement), require a signature if needed, and set the re-sign frequency (e.g., every 365 days).

2.6 Standardizing Sign-In Fields

📋 Goal: To collect consistent data from all visitors across all locations for security and reporting purposes.
11

In the Sign-in flow section, review the fields visitors must complete.

12

Ensure the following fields are enabled and required: Full Name, Company, and Who are you visiting?.

13

Add custom fields only if they are globally applicable. Avoid location-specific fields in the master template.

2.7 Syncing and Verifying the Template (Staged Rollout)

🚀 Goal: To safely deploy the standardized template to all relevant locations and confirm it works as expected.
14

Once all configurations are complete, click "Save template".

15

Phase 1 (Pilot): Apply the template to a small pilot group of 3-5 locations.

  • Choose locations with responsive on-site staff.
  • For comprehensive feedback, select a mix of high-traffic and low-traffic sites, and if applicable, locations in different time zones.
16

Crucial Step: Contact an on-site representative at a pilot location. Ask them to refresh their Envoy iPad App and run a test sign-in to verify all changes.

  • How to Refresh: On the iPad's welcome screen, tap the company logo five times consecutively. A settings menu will appear. Tap the 'Refresh' button.
  • This action forces the iPad to download the latest settings from the Envoy dashboard.

iPad Refresh Troubleshooting:

If changes don't appear after a refresh, try the following:

  1. Force-quit and restart the Envoy app.
  2. Check that the iPad is connected to a stable Wi-Fi network.
  3. As a final step, delete and reinstall the Envoy app from the App Store.
17

Phase 2 (Full Rollout): After 24-48 hours with no issues reported from the pilot group, apply the template to all remaining locations.

18

Communicate Changes: Send a clear communication to all location admins and front desk staff. The email must include:

  • A brief summary of the changes (e.g., "Updated NDA for all visitors").
  • The exact date and time the change will be effective.
  • A screenshot of the new welcome screen for reference.
  • A "What to look for" section, guiding staff to verify specific changes (e.g., "Please confirm the new logo is visible and that the 'Health Screening' question now appears").
  • A point of contact (e.g., the Global Facilities Admin) for any questions or issues.

2.8 Long-Term Template Management & Governance

19

Archiving Old Templates: When a new master template (e.g., v1.5) supersedes an old one (v1.4), archive the old template to keep the dashboard clean. Do not delete it, as you may need to reference it for historical purposes.

  • How to Archive: Navigate to Visitors → Visitor Types → Templates. Find the old template, click the three dots (...) on the far right, and select 'Archive'.
20

Removing a Location: If a location needs to switch to a custom experience, navigate to the master template, click 'Edit,' and deselect the location from the list of applied locations.

Before removing a location, ensure a new custom template is ready and assigned to that location's visitor types to prevent any disruption to the sign-in process. When un-assigned from a master, a location will revert to its previously used template or the Envoy default.
21

Periodic Review Cycle: The Global Administrator should review the master template on a semi-annual basis (every six months).

  • This review should verify that branding is current, legal documents are up-to-date, and sign-in fields align with current company policies and data collection needs.
